subject: Sociology
title: Colonialism's Long Shadow
description: A quick win: understand Colonialism's Long Shadow.

hook.text: Belgium's King Leopold killed ten million Congolese to extract rubber. The Congo is still one of the poorest nations.

buildup.text: Colonialism didn't just take resources. It wrecked local institutions, imposed borders, and created dependency.

discovery.text: Colonial powers drew borders that split ethnic groups and merged rivals, seeding conflicts that persist today.

twist.text: Independence didn't reset the clock. Former colonies inherited debt, broken institutions, and unfair trade deals.

climax.text: Neocolonialism continues through debt structures, resource extraction, and trade agreements written by wealthy nations.

punchline.text: The colonies became nations. The exploitation just changed form.

quiz.question: How does colonialism affect modern inequality?
options: It destroyed institutions and created lasting dependency
It had no long-term effects on former colonies
